dataAvailability.executive: Ukraine remains under martial law since February 2022, extended until May 4, 2026. Constitution allows extension of presidential and parliamentary terms during martial law. All major parties agreed in 2023 to postpone elections until after war ends. Peace negotiations are ongoing but have not yielded breakthrough. Zelensky indicates readiness for elections if security guaranteed by allies within 60-90 days, but this remains contingent on ceasefire agreement. Prime Minister Svyrydenko appointed July 2025, replacing Denys Shmyhal as part of government reshuffle focused on wartime economic management and weapons production.

dataAvailability.executive: Egypt operates as an authoritarian system under President Sisi with systematic repression of genuine opposition. The 2025 parliamentary elections were marred by widespread violations, candidate disqualifications, and low genuine competition, leading to multiple annulments and reruns. Recent protests in 2024 led to mass arrests. Current regional tensions with Iran war affecting energy prices and economic policy. Parliament serves largely as rubber-stamp institution.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Authoritarian system with systematic repression of opposition, limited candidate access, media control, and widespread electoral violations
elections.electionWatchReason: Parliamentary elections concluded January 2026 with results finalized, new parliament formed

lastElection.notes: House of Representatives elections held in phases with multiple runoffs due to widespread violations. Nation's Future Party won approximately 227 seats, Homeland Defenders Party 87 seats. Elections marked by low genuine competition and numerous annulments.
lastElection.runoffCondition: Multiple runoff elections held through January 2026 in constituencies where initial results were annulled

nextElection.notes: Next House of Representatives elections expected around 2030 based on five-year term.

lastElection.notes: Sisi won third term with 89.6% of vote in limited competition against three marginal candidates. Turnout 66.8%.

nextElection.notes: Sisi's third six-year term runs until 2030. Constitutional amendments allow potential further term extensions.

dataAvailability.executive: Cuba operates as a one-party socialist republic where Miguel Díaz-Canel serves as both President and First Secretary of the Communist Party since 2021, making him the supreme leader. Manuel Marrero Cruz has been Prime Minister since December 2019. The Communist Party controls all aspects of governance including candidate selection for elections. The 2023 National Assembly election saw record abstention rates of over 24%, indicating growing discontent, but the system remains non-competitive. Current severe economic crisis includes widespread power outages and fuel shortages exacerbated by U.S. energy blockade following Venezuela situation.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: One-party socialist republic. The Communist Party of Cuba is the only legal party. National Assembly candidates are pre-approved by mass organizations controlled by the Communist Party.
elections.ipu_not_applicable_reason: One-party state where the Communist Party controls all nomination processes and elections are non-competitive by design
elections.electionWatchReason: No elections scheduled in the immediate future. Next National Assembly election not due until 2028.

lastElection.notes: Single-slate election with 470 pre-approved candidates. All candidates elected with required majority.

nextElection.notes: Parliamentary elections scheduled by March 2028 under the same non-competitive system.

lastElection.notes: Díaz-Canel re-elected by newly elected National Assembly. Marrero Cruz reappointed as Prime Minister.

nextElection.notes: President to be selected by newly elected National Assembly following 2028 parliamentary elections.

dataAvailability.executive: Gustavo Petro remains president as of April 2026, serving his constitutionally limited single term until August 2026. His approval rating has fluctuated significantly, reaching lows of 26% in 2023 but rebounding to 54% by March 2026. Colombia faces ongoing tensions with the US under Trump administration, with investigations into Petro's alleged drug ties strongly denied by his government.

elections.electionWatchReason: No election is imminent within 3 days and no recent election result is pending

lastElection.notes: Historic Pact won 25 Senate seats (up from 20) and 42 House seats. Democratic Center won 17 Senate seats (up from 13) and 32 House seats. Turnout was 50.16%, higher than 2022 elections. Campaign marred by over 60 political leader killings.

nextElection.notes: Next congressional elections scheduled for March 2030.

lastElection.notes: Gustavo Petro defeated Rodolfo Hernández in runoff with 50.4% of votes. Turnout 58.17%, highest since 1998. Petro became first leftist president in Colombian history.

nextElection.notes: First round scheduled for May 31, 2026. Leading candidates include Iván Cepeda (Pacto Histórico), Abelardo de la Espriella (far-right), and Paloma Valencia (Democratic Center). Petro constitutionally barred from reelection.

dataAvailability.executive: El Salvador has undergone severe democratic backsliding under Bukele with institutional capture of judiciary and legislature, ongoing state of emergency since March 2022 suspending constitutional rights, mass arbitrary arrests of over 80,000 people, and elimination of effective political competition. Constitutional reforms in July 2025 abolished presidential term limits and eliminated runoff elections. Freedom House rates the country as Partly Free with score declining from 56 to 47 out of 100 between 2023-2025. Multiple international organizations classify El Salvador as having transitioned to authoritarianism or hybrid regime status.

elections.nonCompetitiveReason: Authoritarian consolidation under Bukele has eliminated effective political competition through institutional capture, constitutional manipulation, mass arbitrary arrests, harassment of opposition, and uneven playing field
elections.electionWatchReason: Next elections scheduled for February 2027 with clear electoral processes in place

lastElection.notes: New Ideas won 54 of 60 seats (90% supermajority). Concurrent with presidential election. Electoral reforms favored ruling party through D'Hondt system adoption.

nextElection.notes: Scheduled concurrently with presidential and municipal elections. Term length unchanged at 3 years.

lastElection.notes: Bukele won 84.65% in landslide victory despite constitutional prohibition on consecutive terms. Supreme Court ruling enabled his candidacy.

nextElection.notes: Constitutional reforms approved July 2025 eliminated term limits, extended terms to 6 years, and moved election from 2029 to 2027 to synchronize with legislative elections.
nextElection.runoffCondition: Second round eliminated by constitutional reform

dataAvailability.executive: Serbia classified as electoral autocracy by V-Dem Institute and Partly Free by Freedom House due to systematic advantages for ruling party, media dominance, and declining democratic standards. Despite competitive multiparty elections, international observers report irregularities, voter intimidation, and unequal campaign conditions. Prime Minister Macut is an independent academic nominated by President Vučić as part of the Movement for the People and the State, a new political formation. Student-led protests against corruption have continued since late 2024 following infrastructure collapse in Novi Sad. While elections are competitive, democratic institutions face significant pressure from ruling party dominance.

dataAvailability.executive: Turkmenistan is a totalitarian one-party state where President Serdar Berdimuhamedow nominally holds executive power, but his father Gurbanguly Berdimuhamedow wields supreme authority as Chairman of the People's Council and National Leader of the Turkmen People. The political system permits only government-aligned parties (Democratic Party, Agrarian Party, Party of Industrialists and Entrepreneurs) with no genuine opposition. The People's Council functions as the supreme governing body above the legislature, making Gurbanguly Berdimuhamedow the de facto ruler despite his son holding the presidency.

dataAvailability.executive: Uruguay is a stable multi-party democracy with compulsory voting. President Yamandú Orsi and Vice President Carolina Cosse took office March 1, 2025, after winning the November 24, 2024 runoff election. The fragmented parliament requires consensus-building as no coalition holds a majority in the Chamber of Representatives.

electionDataModel.description: Each country's elections block contains competitiveElections (bool), nonCompetitiveReason (str|null), electionWatchActive (bool — true when an election is within 3 days or results are still pending), and legislative/executive sub-blocks each with lastElection and nextElection. Election objects contain: date, type, notes, runoffDate, runoffCondition. partyProfiles contains orientation, ideology tags, and key platforms for each party currently or recently in power.
